topic: Magnetic decay asymmetry ratio — change-detected re-review (post-hoc paragraph + axial-symmetry caveat additions)
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew 373→1368 (+267%) with new post-hoc-test paragraph; detail_evidence_length grew ~2279→2988 (+31%) with axial-symmetry caveat about toroidal aetheric vortex; sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-09T01:10:03Z; obe_skipped=0 (head non-OBE)
our_argument_summary: Hemispheric magnetic decay asymmetry is adequately explained by standard MHD geodynamo physics (reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary, e.g., Olson & Deguen 2012, Aubert et al. 2013) and accurately measured by CHAOS-7. The dome model claims to match the asymmetry but provides no derivation from dome geometry to a specific numerical ratio. WIN also fails the post-hoc test: CHAOS-7 was a published indexed dataset before the dome adopted it.

relevant_files: raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt (only the WIN-063 trailing paragraph captured for context — full WIN-063 entry not in our raw-text/ corpus)
raw-text/02-wins.txt (V50.x snapshot from 2026-03-12 — predates WIN-063 which appears in V51)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not directly inspectable from our clone — no monitor.py is present anywhere in raw-text/ or the repo, meaning the dome's automation surface for WIN-063 cannot be re-verified from this review run.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Cannot verify from current raw-text. Verdict text's claim 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' is plausible (consistent with monitoring:'none' tag) but should be hedged or substantiated with a fresher capture.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, Earth Planets Space)
MHD simulation literature (Olson & Deguen 2012; Aubert et al. 2013)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome's WIN-063 trailing paragraph does specify a numerical 3:1 asymmetry vs the globe's ~1.0x — but offers no derivation path from disc_radius/firmament_height to that value, only a hand-wavy mechanism ('sub-terrestrial return path exits preferentially through the SAA shoulder').
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Detail_evidence claim '(a) what numerical ratio is claimed' is incorrect — the dome does specify 3:1 in its WIN-063 Dome-vs-Globe paragraph. This is a strawman risk.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py present in our raw-text/ corpus to verify automation; WIN entry visible in 02b-wins-V51-win064.txt only as a trailing paragraph. Verdict text asserts no monitor.py entry exists for this value, consistent with absence of a numerical-ratio domain in the dome's monitor surface as of last full capture.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Hemispheric/SAA decay asymmetry is described and explained in published peer-reviewed literature: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9) describes the field; Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506) and Aubert et al. 2013 (doi:10.1038/nature12574) explain it via reversed-flux patches and core-mantle boundary heat-flux variations. The dome merely renames the cause (toroidal aetheric flow / sub-terrestrial return path).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 model was published 2020; the dome's WIN-063 confirmation date in our wins.json is 2026-04-04 (per surrounding V51 captures), well after CHAOS-7 was an indexed public dataset. Under the legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b ('not plausibly known to the author at write-time'), CHAOS-7 fails the test.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula in dome materials connects disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, or moon_altitude=2534 km to a specific 3:1 hemispheric decay asymmetry. The dome's only cited mechanism is qualitative ('sub-terrestrial return path exits preferentially through the SAA shoulder') with no quantitative path to 3:1.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence (a) reads: 'The dome model states the asymmetry ratio matches without specifying: (a) what numerical ratio is claimed'. The dome's actual WIN-063 trailing paragraph (raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:12) DOES specify a numerical ratio: 'A symmetric globe dipole requires approximately equal decay rates at conjugate latitudes (~1.0x ratio). A 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow.' A dome defender will quote our (a) and the dome's '3:1' side-by-side and accuse us of strawmanning. This is exactly the kind of factual misstatement the curmudgeon exists to catch.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite (a) to acknowledge the 3:1 specification, then attack on the real weakness: the 3:1 is not derived from any of the dome's four geometric parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude). Suggested replacement: 'The dome's trailing paragraph specifies a 3:1 hemispheric asymmetry vs the globe's ~1.0x. But this 3:1 is adopted from CHAOS-7 observation, not derived from dome geometry — no formula on the dome site shows how disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km produce specifically 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1).'

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence asserts 'The dome provides no numerical value, no derivation, and no mechanism.' The dome's WIN-063 Dome-vs-Globe paragraph DOES provide a numerical value (3:1) and offers a qualitative mechanism (sub-terrestrial return path / SAA shoulder). TLDR factual errors are major per Severity Guidelines because TLDRs render before the user expands. A dome defender can dismiss the entire WIN-063 panel by pointing at the TLDR alone.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite tldr_evidence to: 'Hemispheric asymmetry in Earth's magnetic field is modeled by CHAOS-7 from satellite data and explained by reversed-flux patches in the outer core (Olson & Deguen 2012; Aubert et al. 2013). The dome's WIN-063 specifies a 3:1 asymmetry but adopts that ratio from observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry, and offers only a qualitative mechanism (toroidal return-path through the SAA shoulder).' TLDR_verdict has the same problem ('specifies no numerical value') — same fix.

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text asserts 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite.' We do NOT have a current monitor.py capture in raw-text/ to substantiate this. The claim is plausible (consistent with our monitoring:'none' tag and with the broader pattern that ratio-style WINs are often unautomated), but a dome defender can demand the receipt. If the author quietly added a domain to monitor.py that fetches the asymmetry ratio, our verdict text becomes a fabricated negative.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) re-capture monitor.py from the dome repo and confirm the absence of any decay-asymmetry-ratio domain (best); or (b) hedge to 'no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in our last monitor.py capture (date YYYY-MM-DD)'. Do not make unhedged absence claims about content we cannot currently verify.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence (c) — 'using what definition of hemispheric on a flat disc (where the geometric northern hemisphere is a central disc and the southern hemisphere is the surrounding annulus — structurally asymmetric by construction)' — is rhetorically strong but slightly overstated. The dome's text gestures at a definition (geographic latitude / SAA shoulder), which a defender will say IS a definition. The deeper issue — that a flat-disc geometry produces an asymmetric 'hemisphere' tautologically and so the asymmetry ratio is non-comparable to a globe ratio — is the right argument; we should make it explicitly rather than implying the dome has no definition at all.
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe (c) to: 'the dome's flat-disc geometry makes the central-vs-annular regions geometrically non-comparable to a globe's northern-vs-southern hemispheres, so any ratio observed on either model means a different thing — but the dome doesn't address this incommensurability or explain why its inherent geometric asymmetry should produce specifically 3:1 rather than any other ratio.'

holes_found.description: detail_evidence cross-references WIN-005 as having claimed 'the African SAA cell decays faster than the American cell.' WIN-005 in the current wins.json describes it as 'Atlantic cell' rather than 'American cell' (detail_claim: 'African SAA cell decays faster than Atlantic cell'). The two terms are commonly interchangeable in geomagnetic literature (the SAA western cell sits east of the Americas, west of the Atlantic) but exact cross-WIN quotation should match.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'the American cell' to 'the Atlantic cell' in detail_evidence to keep the cross-WIN quote literal. This is a cosmetic fix but matters for citation discipline.

stronger_arguments.description: Make the lack-of-derivation explicit and quantitative. The dome has four free geometric parameters (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, moon_altitude=2534 km). For any of those parameters to predict 3:1 (rather than 2:1, 4:1, or 1.5:1), there must be a derivation. Show the absence of that derivation directly: list the four parameters, then show that no published dome-side formula maps them to a hemispheric decay ratio. This is more diagnostic than the current generic 'no derivation' complaint and ties into the broader cross-page argument about post-hoc parameter fitting.

Connect to WIN-041 and WIN-042 explicitly: those two earned 'Misleading' verdicts for re-slicing CHAOS-7/INTERMAGNET decay statistics from the same dataset. WIN-063 is the same data sliced as a hemispheric ratio. Our current detail_evidence does mention this in passing ('part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063') but a dome defender can still claim WIN-063 is independent. Argue explicitly: 'If we computed the African-vs-American cell ratio from WIN-005's data, we would obtain a similar 3:1. WIN-063 is therefore a derived metric of WIN-005, not an independent prediction.' Then cite WIN-041 and WIN-042 as precedents where this re-slicing logic was already accepted.
sources: review history showing the Misleading verdict pattern

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that hemispheric magnetic decay IS asymmetric — that's a real, measured phenomenon documented in CHAOS-7 (data/wins.json:WIN-005 detail_evidence; doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9). And the dome's rough qualitative claim that 'a perfectly symmetric core flow would produce ~1.0x' is also correct in spirit — a true axisymmetric MHD geodynamo does not naturally produce a 3:1 hemispheric ratio without breaking symmetry somewhere. So the puzzle the dome points at is real (raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:12).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real puzzle is not the same as solving it. Standard physics solved the asymmetric-decay puzzle decades ago via reversed-flux patches and CMB heat-flux variations (data/wins.json:WIN-063 detail_evidence cites Olson & Deguen 2012 doi:10.1038/ngeo1506; Aubert et al. 2013 doi:10.1038/nature12574). The dome model offers only a renamed mechanism (toroidal aetheric return-path through SAA shoulder) and adopts the observed ratio without deriving it from dome geometry. Naming the same puzzle and pointing at the same data does not equal predicting the answer.

deeper_analysis:
The cycle-2 review (2026-04-09) of WIN-063 captured a fingerprint with detail_verdict_length=373 and detail_evidence_length=2279. Between then and 2026-05-09 the verdict text grew by ~995 characters with the addition of a 'post-hoc test' paragraph that invokes the Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out. That addition is correct on its own merits — CHAOS-7 was indexed in 2020, the dome adopted it later, the legitimate-novelty test fails. The Part 3.1b anchor (#posthoc-standard) and the 'plausibly known to the author at write-time' phrasing both currently exist in data/sections.json:part3.html (verified). So the new paragraph is internally consistent.

The more concerning growth is the +31% in detail_evidence, which added the axial-symmetry caveat ('Although the dome's H(r) function is axially symmetric...no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio'). That caveat is good and pre-empts a likely dome rebuttal — but it does NOT pre-empt the simpler rebuttal: 'we said 3:1 in our trailing paragraph, your review says we said nothing.' The caveat answers the wrong question.

The verdict 'Std Model Explains' still holds — the underlying physics is genuinely well-explained by reversed-flux patch geodynamo theory. But the supporting argumentation has a strawman embedded in it that needs to come out before a dome defender finds it. Hence minor_edit, not major_rewrite.

One meta-point: this is a recurring pattern. The decider/analyst pipeline keeps adding nuance to verdict texts to address curmudgeon concerns about post-hoc methodology, but the underlying detail_evidence sometimes has older strawman language that survives unedited. Periodic review of the original detail_evidence against the current dome text is essential — the dome's WIN entries are not frozen artifacts.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender steeped in our six methodological principles would say: 'Your review accuses us of failing to specify a numerical ratio. Read our own WIN-063 Dome-vs-Globe paragraph: we explicitly compare the globe's ~1.0x to the toroidal ovoid's 3:1. We named the mechanism (sub-terrestrial return path through the SAA shoulder). We named the ratio. Your reviewer either did not read past the headline or willfully strawmanned us. By your own internal-consistency-first principle, your review should be retracted on this point or substantially rewritten. Once you remove that strawman, the remaining critique reduces to we adopted a measured value rather than deriving it — which is exactly what you accept Olson & Deguen 2012 doing for the same phenomenon, since their reversed-flux patches are also fitted to the asymmetry, not derived from a more fundamental theory.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three text edits, in priority order: (1) Replace detail_evidence (a) with an acknowledgment of the 3:1 specification followed by the lack-of-derivation attack; (2) Rewrite tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict to remove 'no numerical value' language and replace with 'adopted from observation, not derived from dome geometry'; (3) Add a sentence to detail_evidence directly addressing the 'you accept Olson & Deguen as fitted-not-derived too' rebuttal: 'Olson & Deguen 2012 fit reversed-flux patches to the observed asymmetry, but those patches emerge from MHD simulations using independently measured CMB boundary conditions from seismology — the asymmetry is reproduced from physics, not assumed. The dome's 3:1 is asserted with no comparable physics-from-independent-inputs path.'

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No cross-WIN contradictions in our arguments. The 'American cell' vs 'Atlantic cell' wording inconsistency (noted as a minor hole above) is a quotation-discipline issue, not a substantive cross-WIN inconsistency.

claims_checked: Dome's 3:1 hemispheric decay asymmetry vs globe's ~1.0x
quantitative_verification.computations: Length drift verified: (1368-373)/373 = 2.667 = 266.7%, matches drift-audit.json max_drift_pct of 266.76 (minor rounding). The 3:1 asymmetry ratio itself I cannot verify against CHAOS-7 directly without fetching the model — but the canonical literature values for hemispheric SH-vs-NH dipole-decline ratios from CHAOS-7 are not commonly cited as '3:1' in peer review; the dome appears to be using a custom slice (e.g., African SAA cell decay rate vs an unspecified northern reference) to obtain 3:1. This is itself a finding: the '3:1' is not a standard literature quantity, which strengthens the 'no derivation, custom slice' critique.
